frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: current percepts are biased toward features remembered from prior episodes

frameworks.name: feature binding
frameworks.one_line: content and context features remain bound and carry over across memory episodes

frameworks.name: perceptual stability via temporal smoothing
frameworks.one_line: serial dependence framed as compensation for small cross-episode appearance changes

core_question: Do congruent context features, beyond content similarity, support serial dependence between objects across memory episodes?
key_assumptions: serial dependence promotes perceptual stability across episodes
objects can be differentiated by to-be-memorized content and discriminative context features
manipulations: congruence of context features across successive trials
content similarity between objects across trials
multi-object memory configurations across four experiments
key_findings: stronger serial dependence between objects sharing the same context features across trials
context congruence supports serial dependence in addition to content similarity
effect observed consistently across four experiments
interpretations: content-context binding is not erased but carried over to the next memory episode
selective temporal stability of multiple objects is supported by contextual feature congruence
